title: orchis papilionacea subsp. expansa
intro: Orchis papilionacea subsp. expansa, also known as the butterfly orchid, is a type of orchid native to Europe and Western Asia, characterized by its distinctive purple flowers with a white lip and long, slender leaves. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for centuries, valued for its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which are attributed to the presence of flavonoids and phenolic acids. The health benefits of orchid extracts include improved cardiovascular health, antioxidant activity, and potential anti-cancer properties, although further research is needed to confirm these effects. In herbal preparations, orchid extracts are often used to make teas, tinctures, and infusions, which are believed to offer a range of health benefits when consumed in moderation.
